identity.short_name: Count H\leqG with g\inH and G/H≅(2^5,2^2), mod 1000

subdomain(s): finite abelian groups and invariant factors
quotienting by a fixed cyclic subgroup
topic_path: Finite abelian p-groups and subgroup counts

problem_spec.summary: Given a finite abelian 2-group G in invariant-factor form, a fixed element g\inG, and a target quotient type Q, count subgroups H\leqG such that g\inH and G/H≅Q. Use the kernel-surjection correspondence with the extra constraint to reduce to counting surjections from G/⟨g⟩ onto Q modulo Aut(Q), then reduce the count mod 1000.
givens: H ranges over subgroups of G such that g \in H and G/H ≅ Q.
ask: Compute N = #{H \leq G : g \in H and G/H ≅ Q} and output N (mod 1000).

parameter(s).name: G_invariant_exponents
parameter(s).domain: nonincreasing tuple (a1,...,ar) with ai>=1 defining G≅⊕ Z/2^{ai}Z

parameter(s).name: fixed_element_description
parameter(s).domain: specified element g in G, typically a 2^t multiple of a generator in one summand

parameter(s).name: Q_invariant_exponents
parameter(s).domain: nonincreasing tuple (b1,...,bs) with bj>=1 defining Q≅⊕ Z/2^{bj}Z

invariant(s).name: containment_reduction_to_quotient
invariant(s).statement: Subgroups H\leqG with g\inH and G/H≅Q correspond to kernels of surjections f:G\toQ with f(g)=0, equivalently to surjections G/⟨g⟩\toQ. Hence N = |Epi(G/⟨g⟩,Q)| / |Aut(Q)|.

invariant(s).name: hom_count_depends_only_on_invariant_factors
invariant(s).statement: |Hom(G,Q)| is determined by the exponents via |Hom(Z/2^a,Q)| = |Q[2^a]| = 2^{Σ_j min(a,bj)}.

invariant(s).name: epimorphisms_via_maximal_subgroups_rank2
invariant(s).statement: For rank-2 Q, maximal subgroups have index 2 and correspond to the 3 nonzero lines in Q/2Q≅(Z/2)^2; inclusion-exclusion over these maximal subgroups yields |Epi(G,Q)|.

essential_assumption(s): All groups are finite abelian 2-groups written in invariant-factor form.
The condition g\inH is interpreted literally as subgroup containment.
Aut(Q) acts freely on Epi(G,Q) by postcomposition, so dividing counts kernels.
uniqueness_certificate(s): The constraint g\inH forces reduction to G' = G/⟨g⟩. The kernel of any surjection G'\toQ gives such an H, and two surjections have the same kernel iff they differ by Aut(Q). Since |Epi(G',Q)| and |Aut(Q)| are uniquely determined from invariant factors and maximal-subgroup inclusion-exclusion, N mod 1000 is unique.

technique(s).name: reduce_by_fixed_element
technique(s).purpose: replace the constrained subgroup count by an unconstrained count on G' = G/⟨g⟩

technique(s).name: kernel_surjection_correspondence
technique(s).purpose: convert subgroup counting to counting surjections modulo Aut(Q)

technique(s).name: count_hom_sets_by_invariant_factors
technique(s).purpose: compute |Hom(Gprime,Q)| and similar Hom-counts to maximal subgroups

technique(s).name: epimorphism_count_via_maximal_subgroups
technique(s).purpose: compute |Epi(Gprime,Q)| using inclusion-exclusion over index-2 maximal subgroups of Q

technique(s).name: compute_aut_size_for_rank2_2group
technique(s).purpose: compute |Aut(Q)| by counting endomorphisms with invertible action on Q/2Q

theorem(s).name: first_isomorphism_theorem
theorem(s).role: kernels of surjections correspond to quotient isomorphism types

theorem(s).name: classification_of_finite_abelian_p_groups
theorem(s).role: ensures invariant-factor form and Hom-count formulas

theorem(s).name: maximal_subgroups_via_Q_mod_2Q
theorem(s).role: maximal subgroups of a rank-2 2-group correspond to lines in Q/2Q

theorem(s).name: endomorphism_is_automorphism_iff_mod_2_is_invertible
theorem(s).role: for finite 2-groups, surjectivity is equivalent to surjectivity on Q/2Q (Burnside/Frattini viewpoint)

critical_trick(s): Enforce g\inH by passing to G/⟨g⟩, then count surjections onto Q modulo Aut(Q).
Use inclusion-exclusion over the three maximal subgroups of Q determined by Q/2Q≅(Z/2)^2.

library(s).name: sympy
library(s).reason: exact integer arithmetic and symbolic power-of-2 manipulations (optional)

library(s).name: itertools
library(s).reason: brute-force validation on smaller exponent instances (optional)

task(s).name: implement_hom_and_epi_formulas
task(s).reason: compute Hom-counts and apply inclusion-exclusion for rank-2 quotients

task(s).name: verify_aut_formula_small_cases
task(s).reason: brute-check |Aut(Z/2^a ⊕ Z/2^b)| for small (a,b) to validate the counting rule

task(s).name: mod_1000_reduce
task(s).reason: reduce the final subgroup count modulo 1000
